title: docs: fix contraction in generated error comment
body:
## Summary
- Change `dont` to `don't` in the generated error comment.

## Related issue
- N/A

## Guideline alignment
- Read the contribution guide where present and kept this to one focused text-only file change.
- No behavior, test, fixture, changelog, or generated-file changes.

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- Not run: broader tests are unnecessary for this text-only change.

## AI assistance
- Prepared with Codex and reviewed before submission.
author: Rohan5commit

bot_comments.body: ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 215` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing Rohan5commit:docs/fix-dont-contraction-comment-20260415 (dc9ecd8) with main (895d855)

label_notes: AI-assisted trivial cross-repo doc-fix to wrong repo. Rohan5commit (prior_prs=8, CONTRIBUTOR) submitted +1/-1 change replacing dont → don't in a comment inside pydantic-core/src/errors/types.rs. Critical wrong-repo issue: file path is pydantic-core/... which is a separate repository (pydantic/pydantic-core, the Rust validation core), not part of pydantic/pydantic. PR was submitted to pydantic/pydantic where this file does not exist on main — the change would never have merged here regardless. Body explicit AI disclosure: 'Prepared with Codex and reviewed before submission.' Body has slop-typical formatting: structured headers (Summary/Related issue/Guideline alignment/Validation/AI assistance), 'Read the contribution guide where present' as pseudo-compliance signal, 'Validation: git diff --check' as theatre-tier verification for one-character change. Silent-close 18 minutes after submission, no comments. Same author has 8 prior PRs in pydantic — suggests pattern of AI-assisted trivial-doc-fix submissions trawling for contribution credit. Different from earlier pydantic slop anchors (#13082 maintainer-explicit-AI-rejection, #13083 silent-coverage-gap): here it's cross-repo-wrong-target + AI-checkbox-theatre + trivial-doc-trawl combination. Calibration: AI-disclosure honesty doesn't redeem a wrong-repo PR — Codex generated a real change against a real file path but couldn't reason that the path belonged to a different GitHub repo. Adds: AI-assisted-cross-repo-wrong-target sub-pattern + structured-checkbox-theatre signal.
